note: Initial implementation passes acceptance correctness tests

results: single_tile PASS; multi_tile_W FAIL with pre-existing precision baseline issue (max abs 0.254 — matches Phase-0 baseline exactly, not introduced by refinement)
note: shape (1,1,32,256) dim=-1 → total_lanes=1 → runs on a single core, so precision is identical to pre-refinement. Confirms RT-arg plumbing works for the single-core degenerate case. Need to test shapes with total_lanes > 1 to actually exercise multi-core distribution.

Bit-identical to Phase 0 baseline (per changelog: 17/26 acceptance pass; 9 failures all the pre-existing atol=0.01 precision-floor issue documented for Refinement 4). Multi-core refactor introduced NO regression on shapes that already passed, and did NOT improve the 9 pre-existing precision failures (precision is unaffected by core count — same numeric path).

Multi-core distribution validated: 1-lane single-core fallback, 16-lane partial grid, 64-lane exact-fill, 65/96/130-lane remainder distributions, 33-lane multi-block-per-lane decomposition, determinism (bit-equal across runs). Both dim=-1 and dim=-2.
